name: Etxauri, Alto del Perdón, Balsa Iiazabal – Circular Route
name_original: Etxauri, Alto del Perdón, Balsa Iiazabal – Ruta Circular
description: This difficult 25.5-mile touring cycling route features challenging climbs, technical descents, and panoramic views of Pamplona and the Pyre
region_intro: Touring cycling around Etxauri offers diverse landscapes in the Navarre region of Northern Spain, characterized by mountain ridges, the Arga River valley, and verdant areas with vineyards and orchards. The region features notable natural landmarks such as the Etxauri Cliffs and provides varying elevations suitable for different cycling preferences. Its proximity to Pamplona makes it accessible for cyclists seeking routes with scenic views and varied terrain. Best touring cycling routes around Etxauri The most popular touring cycling route is Etxauri, Alto del Perdón, Balsa Iiazabal – Circular Route, a 25.5 miles (41.0 km) trail that takes 3 hours 12 minutes to complete. This route includes views of the Pilgrim Monument at Alto del Perdón. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Gazebo in Plaza del Castillo – Puente la Reina Romanesque Bridge loop from Echarri / Etxarri, a difficult 58.1 miles (93.4 km) path. This route offers expansive views and passes through areas near Puente la Reina. Local touring cyclists also love the Etxauri Pass loop from Etxauri, a 62.6 miles (100.7 km) trail leading through varying gradients and landscapes, often completed in about 7 hours 5 minutes. Touring cycling around Etxauri is defined by mountain ridges, the Arga River valley, and the distinctive Etxauri Cliffs. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to different ability levels. The routes in Etxauri are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 200 reviews. More than 4000 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Etxauri's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Etxauri?
A: Etxauri offers a wide variety of touring cycling routes, with over 75 options available. These include 8 easy routes, 38 moderate routes, and 31 difficult routes, catering to different skill levels and preferences.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Etxauri?
A: The region's south-facing cliffs allow for outdoor activities almost year-round. However, spring and autumn are generally ideal for touring cycling in Etxauri, as the weather is pleasant and you can avoid the summer heat. The landscape is notably green during these seasons.

Q: Are there any challenging touring cycling routes in Etxauri?
A: Yes, Etxauri is known for its challenging routes with varying gradients and significant elevations. For experienced cyclists, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31876286" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Etxauri Pass loop from Etxauri&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding option, covering over 100 km with more than 2000 meters of elevation gain. Another difficult route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38183870" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gazebo in Plaza del Castillo – Puente la Reina Romanesque Bridge loop from Echarri / Etxarri&lt;/a&gt;, which offers expansive views over a 93 km distance.

Q: What kind of landscapes and views can I expect on touring cycling routes in Etxauri?
A: Etxauri's touring cycling routes traverse diverse landscapes, including impressive mountain ridges like the Sierra de Sarbil, the picturesque Arga River valley, and verdant areas with vineyards and orchards. Cyclists can enjoy panoramic views of the Alloz Reservoir, Goñi Valley, Pamplona, and the distinctive Etxauri Cliffs. The region is notably green for Spain, offering a lush backdrop for your rides.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Etxauri?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes in Etxauri are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A popular example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e968222978" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Etxauri, Alto del Perdón, Balsa Iiazabal – Circular Route&lt;/a&gt;, which is a 41 km loop featuring views of the Pilgrim Monument at Alto del Perdón.

Q: What attractions or landmarks can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: Along the routes, you can encounter several notable attractions. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/654847" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pilgrim Monument at Alto del Perdón&lt;/a&gt; is a significant landmark with its unique 'pilgrim train' art installation. You might also catch views of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4013419" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Artazul Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; or the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4947091" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ubagua Canyon&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features historical sites like the Church of San Lorenzo and the Citadel of Pamplona, which is now a public park.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy the most about touring cycling in Etxauri?
A: The touring cycling routes in Etxauri are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 200 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, the variety of routes catering to different abilities, and the scenic views of mountain ridges, the Arga River valley, and the Etxauri Cliffs.

Q: Are there any facilities for cyclists in the Etxauri area?
A: Yes, some local campsites, such as Camping Etxarri and Aritzaleku, are well-equipped for cyclists. They often provide workshops for repairs, washing areas, self-repair stations, and even bike rental and storage facilities, making them convenient bases for your cycling adventures.

Q: Are there any touring cycling routes that are suitable for beginners or families?
A: While many routes are moderate to difficult, Etxauri does offer easier options suitable for beginners or families looking for a less strenuous ride. There are 8 easy routes available, allowing you to enjoy the scenic beauty without excessive challenge. These routes typically feature less elevation gain and shorter distances.

Q: How accessible is Etxauri for touring cyclists, especially regarding public transport or parking?
A: Etxauri benefits from its convenient proximity to the city of Pamplona, making it relatively accessible. While specific public transport details for reaching trailheads directly might vary, Pamplona serves as a good hub. For those driving, parking is generally available in and around the villages, though it's always advisable to check local regulations. The roads are generally in good condition with minimal traffic, contributing to a pleasant cycling experience.

Q: Can you recommend a touring cycling route that includes a unique viewpoint?
A: Certainly! The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e968222978" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Etxauri, Alto del Perdón, Balsa Iiazabal – Circular Route&lt;/a&gt; not only offers a circular experience but also passes by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3690724" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View from Alto del Perdón&lt;/a&gt;, which provides expansive views of the surrounding landscape and the famous Pilgrim Monument.

Q: Are there any routes that explore less-traveled paths or gravel sections?
A: Beyond traditional road cycling, Etxauri also offers dedicated gravel routes for those seeking more adventurous rides on dirt roads and less-traveled trails. These routes allow you to delve deeper into the natural landscape away from paved roads, offering a different kind of touring cycling experience. An example of a route that might include varied terrain is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6976983" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lizarraga Pass – Arbizu Eco-Camping loop from Etxauri&lt;/a&gt;, which covers a significant distance and elevation, often traversing diverse surfaces.
tips: The structure is 3 meters wide and more than 66 meters long. One of the longest in Spain.
Wonderful route with a wide view between Astrain and Legarda.

On the heights of the Alto del Perdon, and thus in a prominent place, is this "pilgrim train", sometimes also referred to as the "pilgrim caravan" - built in 1996 by the friends of the Way of St. James from Navarro. The figures are worked as silhouettes of rusty iron.
The descent from Alto del Perdón towards Pamplona is much more comfortable than it might seem at first and offers places to rest with views of the nearby city, which make the journey more enjoyable.
It is a simple route and can be done with a gravel, road or MTB bike

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Etxauri, Alto del Perdón, Balsa Iiazabal – Circular Route?
faqs.answer: This route features a diverse and challenging terrain. You'll encounter significant climbs and descents, including a notably steep ascent to Alto del Perdón, which often involves dirt double tracks. The descent from Alto del Perdón is technically difficult due to rocky sections and even some stairs, which may require careful handling or walking for some riders. While some segments are unpaved and demanding, other parts of the route pass through vineyards, offering a mix of surfaces suitable for touring bicycles.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The komoot data suggests a duration of approximately 3 hours and 11 minutes of active cycling. However, given its 'difficult' rating and significant elevation changes, many cyclists might find it takes longer, especially when factoring in stops for views or navigating challenging sections. Routes with over 1,500 feet of elevation gain or taking more than five hours are generally considered hard and require very good fitness.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: The route offers several notable highlights. You'll pass the impressive Pilgrim Monument at Alto del Perdón, an iconic iron sculpture with panoramic views. Other points include the Aguachiquita Bridge, various Views of the fields, a Bench With View of Pamplona, and a scenic Route Through the Vineyards of Bodega Señorío de Otazu.

faqs.question: Is the Etxauri, Alto del Perdón, Balsa Iiazabal – Circular Route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is officially classified as 'difficult' and is not suitable for beginners. It features significant elevation changes, including steep climbs and technically challenging descents with rocky sections. Cyclists should have very good fitness and experience with diverse terrain, including unpaved and potentially difficult sections.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: To enjoy the best conditions and views, spring and autumn are generally ideal. These seasons offer milder temperatures and less extreme weather than summer or winter. The challenging terrain, especially the rocky descent from Alto del Perdón, can become particularly difficult and potentially dangerous in wet conditions, so avoiding rainy periods is advisable.

faqs.question: Where can I park or access the trailhead for this route?
faqs.answer: While specific parking locations are not detailed, the route is a circular one starting and ending near Etxauri. You would typically look for parking in or around the village of Etxauri to begin your ride. Public transport options directly to the starting point are generally limited for such specific routes, so driving to Etxauri is often the most practical option.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is primarily designed for touring bicycles and includes challenging terrain, including steep climbs, rocky descents, and sections that may require walking your bike. While there are no explicit prohibitions against dogs, the nature of the route makes it generally unsuitable for cycling with dogs, especially given the technical difficulties and potential for unpaved sections.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle the Etxauri, Alto del Perdón, Balsa Iiazabal – Circular Route. It uses public trails and roads in the Navarra region of Spain.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other paths. You'll find sections that are part of the "The Pathway of the Arga river", "Iruñerriko ibai-pasealekua", and the "Tour of the Pamplona Basin". It also briefly touches upon parts of the "Camino Francés - 01" and "Donejakue bidea", which are significant pilgrimage routes.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes, pubs, or accommodation options near the route?
faqs.answer: While the route itself is quite challenging and covers varied terrain, you can expect to find cafes, pubs, and accommodation options in the villages and towns near the start/end point, such as Etxauri, or in larger nearby cities like Pamplona. It's advisable to plan stops in advance as options directly on the more remote sections of the route may be scarce.

faqs.question: What kind of views can I expect along the trail?
faqs.answer: The route offers breathtaking panoramic views. From Alto del Perdón, you'll see expansive vistas of Pamplona and the Pyrenees to the north, alongside vast fields of cereals and the historical Valdizarbe Valley to the south. The climb to Etxauri also provides impressive landscapes with steep rock faces and views stretching over Pamplona and the Río Arga valley. You'll also enjoy green expanses and lush forests throughout the ride.
